label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The passage is about flea markets and garage sales in the US. The question specifically asks about garage sales. First, let me recall the relevant part of the text. The passage says: "Perhaps even more enjoyable than flea markets are the weekend outdoor garage sales which have now become very much a part of American culture." So right away, it mentions that garage sales are a part of American culture, which suggests they are popular. That might relate to one of the answer choices. Then it says, "These are held usually in the spring and fall when the weather is still good." So they aren't only in spring, which would eliminate option A. Next: "Usually a homeowner...advertises...that he is holding a garage sale. More than likely the goods on display are in good condition..." So the goods aren't totally useless, which rules out option B. The passage also mentions that if the owner is selling their home or moving soon, they might sell items "at a price far below their true worth." But this is a specific situation, not all garage sales. The question's option D says "All the goods are selling..." which is too absolute. The passage doesn't say all goods are sold below value, only in some cases. So D might not be correct. Looking at the answer choices again: A) Only spring - Incorrect, because the text says spring and fall. B) Goods are totally useless - Incorrect, says goods are in good condition. C) Garage sales
